form_analysis.form_header_text: FY27 Submissions Are OPEN - Appropriations Request
form_analysis.form_instructions_text: The 5th District of Massachusetts is home to many municipalities, organizations, and institutions working on important projects that receive funding from the federal government. As your Member of Congress, I have a unique opportunity to advocate for funding that can create jobs, support scientific research, and improve the lives of families in our district. Programmatic/Bill and Report Language Requests: These types of requests are general funding requests for national and regional programs. Community Project Funding Requests: These types of requests, also known as earmarks, are funding requests for specific community projects. Please limit your requests to one program per submission.

fields.label: Keyword Search
fields.help_text: Search the site for specific terms.
fields.rationale: This is a website search utility and not a form field for the appropriations request.

sections.description: Please provide the contact details for the person submitting this request.

fields.label: Check this box if your organization is located or operates primarily in Massachusetts.

sections.description: Details regarding the requesting organization and its leadership.
Details about the specific funding request and account eligibility.

fields.label: In which Appropriations bill, Agency, Account is this federal program funded?*
options: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies
Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
Labor, Health and Human Services, Education, and Related Agencies
Military Construction, Veterans Affairs, and Related Agencies
State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs
Transportation, Housing and Urban Development, and Related Agencies
fields.draft_value: Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
fields.rationale: NASA's Science Mission Directorate is funded under the CJS subcommittee, as confirmed by the reference values.

fields.label: Request Type*
fields.rationale: The request is for a broad federal program (NASA Science) rather than a specific community project, matching the 'Programmatic' request type in the reference values.

fields.label: Program / Language Title*
fields.rationale: The request is for the Science Mission Directorate (SMD) account as specified in the reference values.

fields.label: Legal name of the entity receiving the funds. (Please do not use abbreviations or acronyms.) *
Requested Funding Action *
fields.rationale: The request is for a specific top-line figure of $9,000,000,000 for NASA Science, which exceeds both the FY2026 enacted level and the known FY2025/2026 budget requests.

fields.label: Exact Funding Amount Requested *
fields.rationale: This reflects the exact funding ask provided in the reference values for the NASA Science account.

fields.label: Total Project Cost
fields.rationale: For a programmatic topline request, the total project cost corresponds to the total account funding requested.

fields.label: What is the minimum amount needed if the full amount requested is not received?
fields.rationale: $7.334 billion represents the FY2025 enacted level, serving as a baseline to prevent further erosion of the science portfolio after recent volatility.

fields.label: Appropriation Request
fields.help_text: Have you submitted this request in a prior year to the Congresswoman's office?
fields.rationale: Whether the submitter has personally submitted this to Representative Clark's office in prior years is not contained in the provided data.

fields.label: If so, which year and how has the project and financing changed?
fields.rationale: This is a user-specific question regarding prior submission history and we cannot provide guidance.

fields.label: What other streams of funding will be used to complete this project?
fields.draft_value:
* NASA Science missions are fundamental public responsibilities under Title 51 of the U.S. Code that no single private entity can replicate
* Direct congressional appropriations serve as the primary funding source for these high-priority national scientific roadmaps
* NASA leverages international partnerships and academic contributions, but federal funding is essential for mission continuity and U.S. leadership
fields.rationale: Frames NASA Science as a primary federal responsibility drawing on advocacy talking points regarding Title 51 and the role of the public sector.

fields.label: Exact Language Requested *
fields.rationale: Provides the specific programmatic funding level requested for the Science account as per the reference data.

fields.label: Please select the account your community project request is eligible for.
options: Department of Agriculture - Farm Production and Conservation Programs, Natural Resources Conservation Service (Conservation Operations)
Department of Agriculture - Agricultural Research Service, Buildings and Facilities
Department of Agriculture - Rural Housing Service, Community Facilities
Department of Agriculture - Rural Utilities Service, ReConnect Grants
Department of Agriculture - Rural Utilities Service, Distance Learning and Telemedicine Grants
Department of Agriculture - Rural Utilities Service, Rural Water and Waste Disposal Grants
Department of Commerce - NIST, Scientific and Technical Research
Department of Commerce - NOAA, Coastal Zone Management
Department of Justice - Byrne Justice Assistance Grants
Department of Justice - COPS Technology and Equipment
National Aeronautics and Space Administration - Safety, Security and Mission Services
Army Corps of Engineers - Mississippi River and Tributaries
Army Corps of Engineers - Operations and Maintenance
Bureau of Reclamation - Water and Related Resources
Federal Emergency Management Agency, Federal Assistance - Pre-Disaster Mitigation Grants
Federal Emergency Management Agency, Federal Assistance - Emergency Operations Center Grants
Environmental Protection Agency - State and Tribal Assistance Grants (STAG), Clean Water State Revolving Fund
Environmental Protection Agency - State and Tribal Assistance Grants (STAG), Drinking Water State Revolving Fund
Military Construction - Air Force and Space Force
Department of Housing and Urban Development - Economic Development Initiatives (EDI)
Department of Transportation - Airport Improvement Program (AIP)
Department of Transportation - Highway Infrastructure Projects
Department of Transportation - Transit Infrastructure Projects
Department of Transportation - Consolidated Rail Infrastructure and Safety Improvements
Department of Transportation - Port Infrastructure Development Program
fields.rationale: This field is only applicable for Community Project Funding (CPF) requests. As this is a programmatic request for NASA Science, this section does not apply.

fields.label: Is the funding recipient a non-profit organization as described under section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, the Commonwealth of Massachusetts, or a unit of local government? *
fields.rationale: While the local recipients (Smithsonian, Harvard, etc.) are non-profits, this field is typically used to determine eligibility for community project funding. For a programmatic request, the 'recipient' is a federal agency (NASA), making the applicability of this field to the submitter uncertain.

fields.label: Can you submit documentation demonstrating community support for this project? *
fields.rationale: NASA Science enjoys broad support from organizations listed in the context, such as the AAS and AGU. Additionally, the district hosts significant activity at the Smithsonian Institution and Harvard, providing a strong basis for community support documentation.

fields.label: Do you consent to publishing the name of the organization or individual requesting funding for this project if Congresswoman Clark submits this request? *
fields.rationale: As a strategic policy advocate (The Planetary Society), public advocacy and visibility are standard practice for programmatic requests.

sections.description: Provide a narrative description of the project and its necessity.

fields.label: Please describe the problem or issue addressed by this request and include pertinent details that justify the need for appropriations. (Character Limit: 2,500)*
fields.draft_value:
* Reverses recent workforce instability and budget disruptions to protect critical aerospace talent at institutions like the Smithsonian Institution in Cambridge
* Maintains U.S. strategic advantage in space against rapid expansion by international competitors, specifically China's lunar and planetary ambitions
* Provides $9 billion to fulfill peer-reviewed Decadal Survey priorities and restore funding levels needed for large-scale mission success
* Sustains over $302M in recent local obligations that drive the MA-5 innovation economy and STEM pipeline
fields.rationale: Combines national strategic concerns (China competition, workforce) with specific MA-5 economic data and recipient relevance.

fields.label: Optional - Please provide any additional information you think will assist in our evaluation of your request. DO NOT copy and paste information previously provided. (Character Limit: 2,500)*
fields.draft_value:
* Direct support for the Smithsonian's operation of the Chandra X-ray Observatory and hourly North American air pollution tracking
* Funding for The Mathworks in Natick and Education Development Center in Waltham for critical engineering software and community geoscience education
* Realizes a 3-to-1 economic return on investment while addressing planetary defense and weather forecasting capabilities
* Advances breakthrough research in solar sail propulsion and advanced infrared instrumentation occurring within the district
fields.rationale: Focuses on specific MA-5 grant and contract recipients (Mathworks, EDC, Smithsonian) and their respective high-tech projects to illustrate district impact.

fields.label: Google Recaptcha Label
fields.rationale: This is a technical field for bot prevention and does not require a drafted response.

issues.field_label: What is the minimum amount needed if the full amount requested is not received?
issues.issue: The draft value of $7,334,000,000 matches the FY2025 Enacted amount, but is not explicitly defined as the 'minimum amount needed' for the current FY2027 request in the fact sheet. Furthermore, the fact sheet indicates the FY2026 Enacted amount was $7,250,000,000, which would typically serve as the floor for a 'no-growth' minimum.

top_contracts.description: Follow on contract to provide operations and science support to the cxo
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Operating and analyzing data from the Chandra X-ray Observatory.

top_contracts.description: Solar b x ray telescope phase E
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Funding long-term operation of a solar X-ray telescope.

top_contracts.description: "igf::ot::igf" other functions - tropospheric emissions: monitoring of pollution (TEMPO) - the tempo investigation was selected under the NASA second stand alone missions of Opportunity notice (SALMON-2) Announcement of Opportunity (AO) program element appendix j, nnh12zda006o-evi1 for the Earth venture instrument-1 (EVI-1) element of the Earth System Science Pathfinder (ESSP) program office'S Earth venture line.  Tempo measures pollution of north america, from mexico city to the canadian tar/oil sands, and from the atlantic to the pacific, hourly and at high spatial resolution. Tempo spectroscopic measurements in the ultraviolet and visible and provides a tropospheric measurement suite that includes the key elements of tropospheric air pollution chemistry. Measurements are from geostationary Orbit, to capture the inherent high variability in the diurnal cycle of emissions and chemistry. A small product spatial footprint resolves pollution sources at sub-urban scale. Together, this temporal and spatial resolution improves emission inventories, monitors population exposure, and enables effective emission-control strategies.  Tempo takes advantage of a geostationary (GEO) host spacecraft to provide a modest cost mission that measures the spectra required to retrieve o3, no2, so2, h2co, c2h2o2, h2o, aerosols, cloud parameters, and uvb radiation. Tempo thus measures the major elements, directly or by proxy, in the tropospheric o3 chemistry cycle. Multi-spectral observations provide sensitivity to o3 in the lowermost troposphere, reducing uncertainty in air quality predictions by 50%. Tempo quantifies and tracks the evolution of aerosol loading. It provides near-real-time air quality products that will be made widely, publicly available.  Tempo makes the first tropospheric trace gas measurements from geo, by building on the heritage of five spectrometers flown in low-earth-orbit (LEO). These leo instruments measure the needed spectra, although at coarse spatial and temporal resolutions, to the precisions required for tempo and use retrieval algorithms developed for them by tempo Science team members and currently running in operational environments. This makes tempo an innovative use of a well proven technique, able to produce a revolutionary dataset.  Tempo provides much of the atmospheric measurement capability recommended for geo-cape in the 2007 national research council decadal survey, Earth Science and applications from space: national imperatives for the next decade and beyond.  Geo-cape is not planned for implementation this decade, however, europe (sentinel 4) and asia (GEMS) will form parts of a global geo constellation for pollution monitoring, with a major focus on intercontinental pollution transport. Tempo will launch at a prime time to be a component of this constellation.
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Tracking hourly North American air pollution for exposure assessment.

top_contracts.description: The tropospheric emissions: monitoring of pollution (TEMPO) contract, will conduct the necessary research, develop algorithms, and provide near real-time products for national aeronautics and space administration (NASA'S) hinod mission.
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Real-time pollution monitoring data development supporting the HINODE mission.

top_grants.description: The ads S mission is aligned with NASA S strategic goal of expanding human knowledge by enabling open science and fostering interdisciplinary breakthrough research.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Developing advanced solar sail propulsion for breakthrough space research.

top_grants.description: We propose to develop a multi-wavelength infrared coronagraph and spectropolarimeter for NASA S scientific balloon program and eventual use in space.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Developing advanced infrared instruments for space observation.

top_grants.description: In october 2020  as part of its strategic goal to support open  interdisciplinary Science  NASA requested the ads team to develop a plan to extend ads'S services to all the scientific disciplines supported by the Science Mission Directorate (SMD). Ads would no longer primarily serve astrophysics (AP) but become the digital library for Planetary Science (PS)  heliophysics (HP)  Earth Science (ES)  and nasa-funded research in Biological and Physical Sciences (BPS).
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Developing the ADS digital library for all NASA science disciplines.

top_grants.description: 23-gio23_2-0010  broadening access to and engagement in geosciences through globe: expanding the mission of the gio
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Expanding community engagement in geoscience education via GLOBE.

top_grants.description: .this is a proposal for a cooperative agreement between NASA Goddard Space Flight Center (GSFC) and the Smithsonian Astrophysical Observatory (SAO) of cambridge  massachusetts. The agreement will continue and expand the extremely productive teaming relationship begun with the hitomi mission and now the xrism mission. The proposed agreement when put in place will ensure that the unique expertise and insight of the sao team will be made available to NASA as part of the us participation in xrism.. Because of the potential for evolution in the us involvement in xrism as well as changes in the needs of the xrism team  the flexibility of a cooperative agreement is in the best interest of both NASA/GSFC and sao. The following subsections outline areas where sao expects to provide important high level and unique support to NASA in formulating and providing the us commitment to the xrism. .this description of areas of participation is not a statement of work. Rather  it defines those areas where it is anticipated that sao can make the most meaningful contribution and  in that sense  shows how sao is able to complement (and supplement) the GSFC team  with all staff working together under the overall direction of the xrism project scientist. .this statement of collaboration outlines how the Smithsonian Astrophysical Observatory (SAO) will carry out its technical  scientific  and management support role in formulation plans for implementing xrism x-ray science in partnership with the xrism project scientist and their team at the NASA Goddard Space Flight Center (GSFC). .the core scientific staff that has supported earlier phases of the hitomi team and now the xrism program  will  under this proposal  continue to support the efforts with GSFC commensurate with the available budgets. 3 requested funding .
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Continuing unique SAO scientific support for the XRISM mission.

top_grants.description: The swift solar activity x-ray imager (SSAXI) instrument for the hi-c flare campaign
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Imaging intense solar flares using the SSAXI X-ray instrument.
